record.field: Mumble is an open-source, low-latency, high-quality voice chat platform that you fully self-host for private and public real-time voice communication.

Mumble is an open-source, low-latency, high-quality voice chat platform that you fully self-host for private and public real-time voice communication.

Mumble pairs a cross-platform client with a self-hosted server (mumble-server, formerly Murmur), built on Qt and the Opus codec. Originally popular for in-game voice, it is equally suited to remote meetings, radio and podcast production, and community voice channels. A single server daemon can run multiple virtual servers, each with its own hierarchical channel tree and fine-grained access control lists.

Key features
  • High-quality, low-latency audio powered by the Opus codec
  • Encrypted by default: TLS control channel and AES-encrypted audio with Perfect Forward Secrecy
  • Hierarchical channels with granular per-channel permissions and access control groups (ACLs)
  • Multiple virtual servers from a single daemon, each on its own port
  • Remote administration via ZeroC Ice and gRPC RPC interfaces
  • Native clients for Windows, Linux, macOS, FreeBSD and OpenBSD
Best for

Teams, communities and organizations that need a private, privacy-respecting voice server they control end-to-end: gaming groups, remote teams, radio/podcast workflows and any deployment requiring scalable, permission-driven voice channels.

services: mumble-server: image: mumblevoip/mumble-server:latest restart: unless-stopped ports: - 64738 - 64738/udp volumes: - ./mumble_data:/data environment: - MUMBLE_SUPERUSER_PASSWORD={{ superuser_password }} - MUMBLE_CONFIG_WELCOMETEXT={{ welcome_text }} - MUMBLE_CONFIG_USERS={{ max_users }} container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_mumble_server' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true
